channel.title: Frontion News
channel.description: Latest Headlines and Strategic Analysis of the Events Reshaping the World

item.title: Pope Leo Calls for 'Weapons to Fall Silent' as Ukraine War Intensifies
item.description: Pope Leo decried the intensification of the war in Ukraine, calling 'for the weapons to fall silent and for the path of dialogue to be followed.' The Pope's appeal comes amid escalating Russian drone attacks and Ukrainian strikes on Russian military infrastructure.

item.title: Russian Strikes Kill Two in Ukraine as Kyiv Hits Russian Drone Factory
item.description: Russian drone attacks killed a 16-year-old boy in Chernihiv and a man in Kherson, while Ukraine's navy struck the Atlant Aero drone factory in Taganrog using Neptune cruise missiles. The facility develops strike and reconnaissance drones and components for UAVs carrying guided bombs.

item.title: Belarus's Lukashenko Says Ready to Meet Trump Once 'Big Deal' Is Prepared
item.description: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko said he would be ready to meet US President Donald Trump once a 'big deal' between the two countries has been prepared. The statement signals potential for Belarus-West diplomacy as the country remains a key Russian ally in the Ukraine conflict.

item.title: IMF and World Bank Meetings Show Limits of Crisis Response Without US Leadership
item.description: Spring meetings of the IMF and World Bank revealed the limits of global institutions in mitigating shocks from the Iran war and other crises, showing that counting on US leadership to resolve global problems is no longer the guarantee it once was. The meetings highlighted a shift in the global financial order.

item.title: Iran Vows to Restrict Strait of Hormuz Ships Until US Blockade Ends
item.description: Iran's parliamentary speaker said Tehran will continue restricting ships through the Strait of Hormuz as long as the US blockade of Iranian ports remains in place, complicating Pakistani-led mediation efforts to extend the ceasefire set to expire Wednesday. The dueling blockades have raised questions about whether the two-week truce can survive.

item.title: Spain and Mexico End Diplomatic Spat Over Colonial Past With Handshake
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum laid to rest a years-long diplomatic dispute with Spain over the Spanish colonial past during her visit to Barcelona. The reconciliation follows King Felipe VI's March acknowledgment of abuses during the conquest of the Americas, ending tensions that began in 2019.

item.title: EU Leaders Back Major New Defense Spending Plans at Ukraine Summit
item.description: European Union leaders committed to a massive step in defense cooperation following decades of hesitation, spurred by the Ukraine conflict and uncertainty about US commitment to NATO. The summit marks a significant shift toward European defense autonomy.

item.title: US Backs South Africa Rare Earths Project to Counter China's Mineral Dominance
item.description: The Trump administration is supporting a $50 million project to extract rare earth elements from mining waste in South Africa, part of accelerated US efforts to reduce reliance on China for minerals crucial to electronics, defense systems, and electric vehicles. The project continues despite a major diplomatic rift between Washington and Pretoria.

item.title: Pope Leo Downplays Feud With Trump, Says 'Not in My Interest' to Debate
item.description: Pope Leo XIV sought to downplay his feud with US President Donald Trump, saying reporting about his comments during his Africa tour 'has not been accurate in all its aspects.' The Pope said it is 'not in my interest at all' to debate Trump but will continue preaching peace.

item.title: UK Seeks EU Reset as Iran War Strains Ties With Trump's America
item.description: Britain's government is set to announce legislation next month to move the country closer to the European Union, as the Iran war and Trump's unpredictability sour the UK's 'special relationship' with the United States. Prime Minister Keir Starmer's EU 'reset' bill would give ministers powers to align UK standards with EU single market rules.

item.title: Mediation Fails in Dispute Over Franco-German Fighter Jet Project
item.description: Mediators have failed to resolve a dispute between companies involved in the Franco-German FCAS fighter jet project, Handelsblatt reported. The setback threatens the Future Combat Air System program, a flagship European defense collaboration aimed at developing next-generation fighter aircraft.

item.title: Turkey Warns US Withdrawal From European Security Would Be 'Destructive'
item.description: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan said discussions are underway on managing a possible US withdrawal from the European security architecture, warning such a move would be 'destructive' for regional stability. The comments reflect growing concern among NATO allies about America's commitment to the alliance.

item.title: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Again as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
item.description: Iran has once again closed the Strait of Hormuz, blaming US 'breaches of trust' for the reversal just one day after announcing the waterway would reopen. IRGC gunboats fired on tankers attempting to pass through, while Washington and Tehran remain 'far from a final agreement' on nuclear talks.

item.title: Two Israeli Soldiers Killed in Southern Lebanon Despite Ceasefire
item.description: Two Israeli soldiers have been killed in southern Lebanon over two days, bringing the IDF death toll to at least 15 since the conflict began. The casualties come despite a 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah, with 12 other soldiers wounded in the incidents.

item.title: South Africa Central Bank Chief Says Iran War Clouds Rate Cut Prospects
item.description: South Africa's central bank governor told Reuters that it was difficult to see a near-term path for easing interest rates due to volatility from the war in the Middle East and its impact on inflation. The comments highlight how the Iran conflict is rippling through global monetary policy.

item.title: Bulgaria Bombarded by Pro-Russian Propaganda Ahead of Elections
item.description: Bulgaria is being flooded with pro-Russian propaganda ahead of Sunday's parliamentary election, with around 600 pro-Kremlin websites spreading disinformation. The campaign comes as polls show pro-Russian former President Rumen Radev as the frontrunner.

item.title: Humanoid Robot Beats Human Record in Beijing Half-Marathon, Showcasing China's Tech Advances
item.description: A Chinese-made humanoid robot completed a half-marathon in Beijing faster than the human world record, finishing in 50 minutes and 26 seconds. The race featured dozens of robots demonstrating rapidly improving athleticism and autonomous navigation skills, highlighting China's advances in humanoid robotics.

item.title: Hungary's Election Winner Magyar Increases Parliamentary Majority After Final Vote Count
item.description: Hungarian election winner Peter Magyar's Tisza party increased its parliamentary supermajority to 141 seats out of 199 after final vote counting. The result solidifies the defeat of Viktor Orbán's Fidesz party and marks a significant shift in Hungarian politics after 14 years of illiberal rule.

item.title: Brazil, Mexico and Spain Pledge Coordinated Aid to Cuba Amid US Embargo Pressure
item.description: The governments of Brazil, Spain and Mexico vowed to step up coordinated aid to Cuba to alleviate what they described as a humanitarian crisis caused by the US blockade of the Caribbean island. The pledge came during a progressive summit in Barcelona that drew condemnation from President Trump.

item.title: British Foreign Secretary Calls for Full Resumption of Hormuz Shipping
item.description: British Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper said the Strait of Hormuz had yet to return to normal operations despite a ceasefire in the Iran war, urging Tehran to allow global shipping to fully resume. The call comes as vessels continue to face uncertainty in the vital waterway.

item.title: Ukrainian Missiles Strike Russian Drone Plant in Taganrog
item.description: A drone manufacturing plant in the southern Russian city of Taganrog caught fire after being struck by Ukrainian missiles overnight. The strike represents Ukraine's continued targeting of Russian military infrastructure as the conflict enters its third year.

item.title: Russia Strikes Zaporizhzhia Region 756 Times in 24 Hours, One Injured
item.description: Russian forces carried out 756 strikes on 44 settlements in Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia region over 24 hours, injuring at least one person. The bombardment continues the pattern of intense Russian attacks on southeastern Ukraine despite ongoing conflict.

item.title: Chernobyl's Wildlife Thrives Four Decades After Nuclear Disaster
item.description: Four decades after the nuclear disaster at Ukraine's Chernobyl power plant, wildlife is thriving again in the exclusion zone. Przewalski's horses and other species have returned to the radioactive landscape, demonstrating nature's resilience in areas now too dangerous for humans.

item.title: Bulgaria Votes as Pro-Russian Former President Leads Polls in Eighth Election in Five Years
item.description: Bulgarians head to the polls for the eighth time in five years, with pro-Russian former President Rumen Radev leading the race. The vote comes after mass protests brought down the conservative-led government in December, with voters frustrated by endemic corruption and weak coalition governments.

item.title: China Positions Itself as Pillar of Stability as US Credibility Declines
item.description: Beijing is capitalizing on US missteps in the Iran war to improve its global image, positioning China as a pillar of stability in contrast to American volatility. The contrast between China's orderly Five-Year Plan approach and US disruption is reshaping how the world views the two superpowers.
